title: PM-Bench: Evaluating Prospective Memory in LLM Agents
acquisition_purpose: Primary-source acquisition for queued review-pmbench-prospective-memory-validity; this record does not claim a deep review.

paper.withdrawal_status: The immutable v1 API summary contains no withdrawal or retraction notice.
paper.extraction_status: Full immutable 22-page v1 PDF extracted successfully. The title and abstract, benchmark construction and validation, evaluation protocol and results, references, appendix prompts, channel-level table, failure cases, and final physical PDF page were inspected for continuity.

official_release.paper_link_evidence: The immutable paper links the PMBench GitHub repository in footnote 1. The queued review specifies the exact commit below.
official_release.paper_release_timing: The pinned commit predates immutable arXiv v1 publication by approximately 12 minutes on 2026-07-14 and is a timing-appropriate snapshot for paper-release correspondence checks.
official_release.license: No repository-level license file was present in the pinned 172-file archive; redistribution and reuse rights are therefore unverified.
official_release.inspectable_artifacts: The pinned archive contains the deterministic v9 scenario and generator, benchmark validator/runner/scorer, eight configuration implementations, 64 released scored trajectories and aggregate report, requirements, and a browser-only human evaluation interface.
official_release.correspondence_boundary: Acquisition and spot inspection establish only that the pinned snapshot is locally complete and contains the release classes named in its README. Reproducing scenario validation, scores, paper tables, costs, or treatment correspondence remains work for the deep reviewer.

claim_boundary: This acquisition establishes local, hash-pinned paper and release evidence only. It is not a deep review and does not validate intention authority, prospective-memory construct validity, scenario solvability, score correctness, configured-treatment comparability, run reliability, transport to real workflows, professional validity, general memory capability, production fitness, or readiness.
